an8.66:1.0.1: "Bhikkhus, there are these eight emancipations.What eight?
an8.66:1.0.2: (2) "One not percipient of forms internally sees forms externally. This is the second emancipation.
an8.66:1.0.3: (3) "One is focused only on ‘beautiful.’ This is the third emancipation.
an8.66:1.0.4: (4) "With the complete surmounting of perceptions of forms, with the passing away of perceptions of sensory impingement, with non-attention to perceptions of diversity,perceiving‘space is infinite,’ one enters and dwells in the base of the infinity of space. This is the fourth emancipation.
an8.66:1.0.5: (5) "By completely surmounting the base of the infinity of space,perceiving‘consciousness is infinite,’ one enters and dwells in the base of the infinity of consciousness. This is the fifth emancipation.
an8.66:1.0.6: (6) "By completely surmounting the base of the infinity of consciousness,perceiving‘there is nothing,’ one enters and dwells in the base of nothingness. This is the sixth emancipation.
an8.66:1.0.7: (7) "By completely surmounting the base of nothingness, one enters and dwells in the base of neither-perception-nor-non-perception. This is the seventh emancipation.
an8.66:1.0.8: (8)"By completely surmounting the base of neither-perception-nor-non-perception, one enters and dwells in the cessation of perception and feeling.This is the eighth emancipation.
